summary: iOS | FALCON | Custom Button setting is not properly refreshed after disconnecting headset from PC
scenario: Custom Button setting not refreshed after disconnecting headset from PC, still shows MS Teams as connected until app restart
description_clean: Custom Button setting is not properly refreshed after disconnecting the headset from a PC. After plugging the headset into a PC with Teams, the custom button settings are locked to Teams. When the headset is unplugged from the PC, the custom button setting is not refreshed to reflect the change in available options — it still shows MS Teams as connected. Only restarting the app restores the ability to change the custom button to other providers.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth headset is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Headset Name] → Custom Button.
4. Verify that the custom button can be assigned to a provider other than Microsoft Teams (e.g., default or another assistant).
5. Connect the headset to a PC running Microsoft Teams via USB cable.
6. While the headset is connected to the PC, observe the Custom Button setting in the Poly Studio Mobile app — it should show Microsoft Teams as the assigned provider and be locked.
7. Disconnect the headset from the PC (unplug USB cable).
8. Return to the Custom Button setting in the Poly Studio Mobile app without restarting the app.
9. Observe the current state of the Custom Button setting.
expected: After disconnecting the headset from the PC, the Custom Button setting should refresh to show available options (e.g., default, other assistants) and allow the user to change the assignment away from Microsoft Teams.
actual: After disconnecting the headset from the PC, the Custom Button setting still shows Microsoft Teams as connected and locked. The available options are not refreshed. Only restarting the Poly Studio Mobile app restores the ability to change the custom button to other providers.
